interfaces: HTTP API on port 3000 (Express routes — no source code present, inferred from Dockerfile CMD node server.js)
Sequelize ORM data access layer (database interactions)
controls: Sequelize ORM (Partial — parameterized queries available but raw query usage unknown)
All other security controls: Missing (no auth, no session, no RBAC, no CSRF, no helmet, no CORS, no rate limiting, no input validation, no TLS, no logging)
known_vulns: lodash@4.17.10: CVE-2019-10744 (prototype pollution via _.merge, High severity)
lodash@4.17.10: CVE-2020-8203 (prototype pollution via _.zipObjectDeep, High severity)

interfaces: npm install during build (supply chain ingress)
Base image pull from Docker Hub (node:20-alpine)
controls: Docker isolation (Partial — container runs as root, reducing isolation effectiveness)
known_vulns: Dockerfile: no USER directive — runs as root (IF-002 VIOLATION)
Dockerfile: mutable base image tag node:20-alpine (IF-007 violation)
Dockerfile: npm install without --ignore-scripts (supply chain risk)
No lockfile: supply chain integrity unverifiable (SC-002 violation)
supply_chain_findings: npm install without --ignore-scripts allows lifecycle hook execution during build
No package-lock.json — versions not pinned, dependency confusion possible
Base image FROM node:20-alpine uses mutable tag — image could be swapped
No Dependabot or Renovate configured — no automated dependency updates
No CI/CD pipeline (no .github/workflows/) — no automated SCA, SAST, or image scanning
